title: Jerotes Village‘s Pillage
description: Added more, the raiders originally belonged to Jerotes village, and any village matel item
body:
**Warning**: Since all creatures in this mod originate from Jerotes Village, and the values of these creatures in Jerotes Village were designed with endgame players (those with level 30 enchanted diamond gear and above) in mind, this mod has **【NEVER MADE ANY NUMERICAL ADJUSTMENTS】** when incorporating them. Please prepare yourself mentally when facing these creatures.

This mod independently transplants 30 raiders from Jerotes Village, along with 4 related animals and 4 related structures, into the Overworld. Among these raiders are two bosses.

Additionally, this mod also retains the Carved Iron Golem and most Village Metal items to facilitate players' early-game exploration. If you need a version that is compatible with Jerotes Village but removes these contents more thoroughly, try JV's Pillage!
﻿
![Raiders](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1b7d18cbe2e2ea244392a1cc2272f3734664c6fc.png)﻿

By default, 16 types of raiders can appear in Village Raids.
﻿
- Defector: Illagers forced to fight you by magical brands. They will only attack you if other raiders are nearby, and may even miss their attacks. Use emeralds on them to set them free.﻿
- Javelin Thrower: Illagers wielding javelins. Their thrown javelins can disable your shield, but they only carry six—one of which is used for melee combat.﻿
- Executioner: Tall illagers wielding two-handed weapons. They can block like players do with large axes and hammers, halving most frontal damage.﻿
- Mapmaker: Illagers that use support magic to make other illagers move faster, quickly closing in on you! Defeating them drops a Second Rounder Map, which can be used to craft magic maps that locate the mod’s four structures.﻿
- Blaster: Illagers armed with bombs and scimitars. Be careful—the bombs they throw explode into fragments!﻿
- Spirve: Undead creatures twisted in mind and body by the Grandmother, wielding strange weapons as they take to the battlefield.﻿
- Cohort Hag: Ugly hags that wield a variety of spells. Once more than three of these creatures from the Feywild gather together, they will use even more varied and deadly magic.﻿
- And there are even more raiders that take part in village raids…

![Ominous Banner Raid Force](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f125565992ce3a0e34ccd2c2afa9137e1c42ee88.png)
 
Use the Ominous Banner Raid Tag Deployer to summon an Ominous Banner Projection and initiate a special “Raid.” In this raid, in addition to 11 types of illagers that can already appear in village raids, 11 special raiders will also spawn—some with unique spawn conditions waiting for you to discover.

![Grandmother and Her Pursuivant](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a7c9e853a22cd08b39fc844defe11b77b23aaa2e.png)
 
Find the Witch Residence atop badlands, defeat the most powerful witch inside, and bring out the Hag’s Cauldron. Find a suitable location, place the cauldron, and add the Evil Potion to summon a projection of the Grandmother, the master of the Feywild: the Purple Sand Hag. After defeating her, use the Hag’s Brush to clean the Hag’s Cauldron. Then, use specific items on it to trade with the Grandmother for special items—whether building materials or powerful potions. You can look up the recipes using JEI.
